title: Boot, Partition, Install, Upgrade, Migration, and Recovery · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic boot, partition, install, upgrade, migration, and recovery ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reversible operating-system deployment runbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory compatibility, support, data, encryption keys, applications, accounts, drivers, firmware, licenses, network, power, access needs, backup, and rollback before change.
Trace firmware mode, Secure Boot, device order, boot manager, loader, recovery environment, signed media, architecture, and trusted source.
Plan partition tables, system and recovery partitions, volumes, filesystems, labels, mount points, encryption, capacity, and data-preserving boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Postdeploy Verify
steps.instruction:
Order the Postdeploy Verify CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Postdeploy Verify.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Postdeploy Verify assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Postdeploy Verify, keep Postdeploy Verify connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Postdeploy Verify has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Postdeploy Verify, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Postdeploy Verify precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Postdeploy Verify, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Postdeploy Verify, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Reaching the desktop proves an operating-system deployment is complete.

options.text: Declare CompTIA A+ support user outcome, fictional ticket, asset and environment identity, authorization, hazards, accessibility and privacy needs, baseline, and stop conditions for the debug Postdeploy Verify case
Predict CompTIA A+ support reproduction, timeline, competing hypotheses, bounded test outcomes, evidence quality, escalation, and rollback triggers for the debug Postdeploy Verify case
Run the smallest deterministic CompTIA A+ support manifest review and retain the actual observation, rejected inference, next decision, and remaining uncertainty for the debug Postdeploy Verify case

Change one CompTIA A+ support user, device, version, hazard, symptom, access need, or competing cause; verify repair, rollback, original and changed cases, prevention, handoff, and controlled equipment transfer limits for the debug Postdeploy Verify case

stimulus.title: Deployment Readiness case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory compatibility, support, data, encryption keys, applications, accounts, drivers, firmware, licenses, network, power, access needs, backup, and rollback before change. Artifact under review: reversible operating-system deployment runbook.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Deployment Readiness, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Deployment Readiness.

For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Postdeploy Verify, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Postdeploy Verify precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Postdeploy Verify, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

For CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Postdeploy Verify, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Reaching the desktop proves an operating-system deployment is complete.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Postdeploy Verify
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Postdeploy Verify: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Postdeploy Verify.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Postdeploy Verify, keep Postdeploy Verify connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Postdeploy Verify, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Postdeploy Verify precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Postdeploy Verify, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: An encrypted fleet deployment changes firmware, boot media, partitions, drivers, apps, profiles, access settings, licenses, and rollback at once. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify boot, activation, updates, drivers, identity, encryption, applications, user data, accessibility, security baseline, backups, recovery, and changed-device behavior.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Postdeploy Verify, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Reaching the desktop proves an operating-system deployment is complete.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
